name: Ala Moana Beach Park Playground
tagline: Huge playground right next to the calmest beach in Honolulu — the ultimate combo for families.
parking: Free parking lot in the park (fills fast on weekends — arrive before 10am)
hours: Park open 4am–10pm daily; playground always accessible during park hours
bestTime: Weekday mornings for parking and shade; late afternoon for cooler play
talkStory: Built on a reclaimed coral reef flat in the 1930s as a WPA project, this 100-acre park was engineered with a protective outer reef that creates one of Hawaii's safest swimming areas -- the water stays ankle-to-knee deep for nearly 100 yards out, which is why local families call it 'the baby beach.' The playground was renovated with ADA-accessible structures and shade sails. Insider move: park at the less-known west end near the channel for easy spots even on weekends.
